title: Radio frequency device
abstract: A radio frequency device (4) comprises a first substrate layer (2) and a second substrate layer (3) arranged at a distance towards each other, whereby the first and second substrate layer (2, 3) comprise electrically conductive transmission line elements (10, 11) on a first surface (6) of the first substrate layer (2) and on a second surface (8) of the second substrate layer (3) that allow for transmission of a radio frequency signal along a transmission direction. The radio frequency device (4) also comprises more than one electrically conductive crossover (1) between the first surface (6) of the first substrate layer (2) and the second surface (8) of the second substrate layer (3) that provides for an electrically conductive connection of the respective electrically conductive transmission line elements (10, 11) on the first and second substrate layer (2, 3). At least one phase shifting region of the radio frequency device (4) comprises corresponding regions of the respective first and second substrate layers (2, 3) that are used for forming at least one radio frequency phase shifting element arranged on the first and second substrate layer (2, 3). All electrically conductive crossovers (1) are arranged outside of the at least one phase shifting region of the radio frequency device (4), whereby each electrically conductive crossover (1) is electrically connected to a respective phase shifting element.
claims:
Claims C l a i m s 1. Radio frequency device (4) comprising a first substrate layer (2) and a second substrate layer (3) arranged at a distance towards each other, whereby the first and second substrate layer (2, 3) comprise electrically conductive transmission line elements (10, 11, 10', 11') on a first surface (6) of the first substrate layer (2) and on a second surface (8) of the second substrate layer (3) that allow for transmission of a radio frequency signal along a transmission direction parallel to the first surface (6) of the first substrate layer (2) or parallel to the second surface (8) of the second substrate layer (3), and with an electrically conductive crossover (1) between the first surface (6) of the first substrate layer (2) and the second surface (8) of the second substrate layer (3) that provides for an electrically conductive connection of electrically conductive transmission line elements (10) on the first surface (6) of the first substrate layer (2) with the electrically conductive transmission line elements (11) on the second surface (8) of the second substrate layer (3), characterized in that more than one electrically conductive crossover (1) is arranged between the first and second substrate layers (2, 3) . 2. Radio frequency device (4) according to claim 1, characterized in that at least one phase shifting region (12) of the radio frequency device (4) comprises corresponding regions of the respective first and second 

surfaces (6, 8) of the first and second substrate layers (2, 3) with electrically conductive transmission line elements (10', 11') that are used for forming several radio frequency phase shifting elements (20) that are arranged inside of a boundary of the at least one phase shifting region (12), and in that all electrically conductive crossovers (1) are arranged outside of the at least one phase shifting region (12) of the radio frequency device (4), whereby each electrically conductive crossover (1) outside of the at least one phase shifting region (12) is electrically connected to a respective phase shifting element (20) inside of the at least one phase shifting region ( 12 ) . 3. Radio frequency device (4) according to claim 2, characterized in that a number of electrically conductive crossovers (1) are arranged along a straight line along a border of the phase shifting region (12) of the radio frequency device (4) . 4. Radio frequency device (4) according to claim 2, characterized in that a number of electrically conductive crossovers (1) are arranged along several straight lines along a border of the phase shifting region (12) of the radio frequency device (4) . 5. Radio frequency device (4) according to claim 4, characterized in that the respective electrically conductive crossovers (1) of a first straight line are arranged at a distance in direction of the first straight line with respect to the adjacent electrically conductive crossovers (1) of a second straight line. 6. Radio frequency device (4) according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that an electrically conductive crossover (1) comprises a first crossover electrode (5) on the first substrate layer (2) and a second crossover electrode (7) on the second substrate layer (3), whereby the first crossover electrode (5) on the first substrate layer (2) at least partially overlaps the second crossover electrode (7) on the second substrate layer (3) . 7. Radio frequency device (4) according to claim 6, characterized in that an electroconductive material (9) is arranged between at least a part of the overlapping area of the first and second crossover electrode (5, 7), whereby the electroconductive material (9) electrically connects the first crossover electrode (5) on the first substrate layer (2) with the second crossover electrode (7) on the second substrate layer (3) . 8. Radio frequency device (4) according to claim 7, characterized in that the electroconductive material (9) comprises electroconductive particles dispersed within a nonconductive matrix material. 9. Radio frequency device (4) according to claim 7, characterized in that the electroconductive material (9) comprises electroconductive particles with a large enough mean diameter to provide for an electroconductive contact with the first crossover electrode (5) and with the second crossover electrode (7) . 10. Radio frequency device (4) according to claim 6 to 9, characterized in that the first crossover electrode (5) of the first substrate layer (2) and/or the second crossover electrode (7) on the second substrate layer (3) is covered by an electroconductive mechanical protection layer (15, 16) . 11. Radio frequency device (4) according to claim 10, characterized in that the electrically conductive crossover (1) is formed by electroconductive material of the mechanical protection layer (15, 16) that electrically connects the first crossover electrode (5) on the first substrate layer (2) with the second crossover electrode (7) on the second substrate layer (3) . 12. Radio frequency device (4) according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that a sealant (18) surrounds either some or each of the electrically conductive crossovers (1), or in that a sealant surrounds some part or all of the region comprising the electrically conductive crossovers (1), or in that a sealant surrounds some part or all of the phase shifting region (12) .
